title: Add regression coverage for interrupted in-progress and checking flows
goal: Add regression coverage proving that interrupted in-progress and checking states resume deterministically from the minimal canonical continuation contract without relying on optional metadata, heuristic selection, or session-led recovery.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 redesign is only complete if interrupted non-terminal work resumes correctly in practice. This ticket validates the earliest non-terminal lifecycle states, where implementation work and checking work are most likely to be interrupted before verify or commit completes.
scope: Add regression tests for interrupted `in-progress` transactions.
Add regression tests for interrupted `checking` transactions.
Test resume behavior when materialized state is healthy and complete.
Test resume behavior when materialized state must be consulted before deciding whether to continue work.
Test that continuation is driven by canonical `next_action` rather than by status-derived heuristics alone.
Test that no-active baseline behavior is not confused with interrupted non-terminal work.
Test that optional phase-style or step-style metadata is not required for successful continuation.
out_of_scope: Interrupted verify-pass, verify-fail, commit, or post-commit flows.
Historical compatibility and malformed persistence coverage beyond what is minimally needed to support these tests.
Changing runtime semantics unrelated to test enablement.
inputs: Outputs and implementation changes from p1-t02, p1-t03, p2-t02, p2-t03, p2-t04, and p2-t05
The runtime test suite and any existing transaction or resume test helpers
expected_outputs: Automated regression tests covering interrupted in-progress resume behavior.
Automated regression tests covering interrupted checking resume behavior.
Fixtures or helpers, if needed, for constructing canonical interrupted states using the minimal continuation contract.
A short note in test code or adjacent comments describing what each interruption scenario is asserting.

scenarios.name: interrupted_in_progress_with_healthy_materialized_state
scenarios.description: A transaction is interrupted while `status` is `in-progress`, `active_tx` is present, and canonical materialized state is healthy. Resume must continue the exact active transaction using canonical `next_action`.

scenarios.name: interrupted_checking_with_healthy_materialized_state
scenarios.description: A transaction is interrupted while `status` is `checking`, `active_tx` is present, and canonical materialized state is healthy. Resume must continue the exact active transaction using canonical `next_action`.

scenarios.name: in_progress_does_not_fall_back_to_begin
scenarios.description: A non-terminal interrupted in-progress transaction must not be treated as no-active work and must not trigger `tx.begin`.

scenarios.name: checking_does_not_fall_back_to_begin
scenarios.description: A non-terminal interrupted checking transaction must not be treated as no-active work and must not trigger `tx.begin`.

scenarios.name: continuation_does_not_require_optional_metadata
scenarios.description: Resume succeeds when phase-style or step-style metadata is absent, as long as the minimal canonical continuation contract is present and valid.

scenarios.name: exact_active_identity_is_preserved
scenarios.description: Resume continues the exact active transaction identity and does not mint or infer a replacement transaction.

assertions: Resume selects the exact `active_tx` from the canonical materialized continuation contract.
Resume does not start a new transaction when interrupted non-terminal work exists.
Resume uses canonical `next_action` as the continuation dispatcher.
Resume does not require phase-style or step-style metadata.
Resume does not rely on sentinel transaction identifiers.
Resume does not rely on timestamp-first or heuristic active transaction selection.
The no-active baseline is not confused with interrupted non-terminal work.
acceptance_criteria: The automated test suite contains regression cases for interrupted `in-progress` and `checking` states.
Each test proves that resume continues the same active transaction rather than creating or selecting a substitute transaction.
Each test proves that canonical `next_action` drives continuation behavior.
At least one test proves that optional phase-style or step-style metadata is unnecessary for continuation correctness.
At least one test proves that interrupted non-terminal work is not treated as a no-active baseline.
The added tests fail against behavior that uses begin-fallback, heuristic selection, or non-canonical dispatch, and pass with the 0.6.0 runtime behavior.
review_checkpoints: Verify that each test constructs or reaches an actually interrupted non-terminal state rather than a synthetic terminal or no-active baseline.
Verify that assertions check exact active transaction continuity, not just generic success.
Verify that tests exercise canonical top-level fields from the minimal continuation contract.
Verify that test names and comments distinguish lifecycle classification (`status`) from continuation dispatch (`next_action`).
notes: This ticket should validate the earliest interruption points in the redesigned work loop before later tickets expand coverage to verify, commit, and terminal flows.
Prefer scenario-based tests that expose protocol regressions clearly over broad end-to-end cases with ambiguous failure causes.
If existing helpers assume phase-style, step-style, session-primary, or heuristic recovery behavior, they should be bypassed or updated so these tests reflect the 0.6.0 protocol directly.
